responseMessageContents.text:
The user is asking me to share a "safety box" to input their GitHub URLs and GitHub Token. They seem to want to provide GitHub credentials securely.

This is a situation where I should use the `requestEnvs` tool to collect environment variables from the user. They want to provide:
1. GitHub URLs (presumably repository URLs)
2. GitHub Token

However, looking at the context more carefully, I think the user might be confused about what I can do. The previous conversation was about creating an iOS app called "Ivx". The user hasn't yet described what the app should do.

The user is asking about sharing GitHub URLs and a GitHub Token. In Rork, the way to securely provide secrets and configuration values is through the `requestEnvs` tool. Let me present a form for them to fill in their GitHub-related credentials.

Let me think about what specific environment variables would be useful:
- A GitHub personal access token (for accessing private repos, etc.)
- GitHub repository URLs

But wait - what does the user actually want to

do with these? They mentioned "safety box" which might refer to a secure input widget. The `requestEnvs` tool creates a widget where they can safely input secrets.

Let me just create the request for the GitHub token and URL(s).

envs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
envs.description: Your GitHub personal access token (classic or fine-grained)

envs.name: GITHUB_REPO_URLS
envs.description: Your GitHub repository URLs (comma-separated if multiple)

input.reason: You asked to share your GitHub URLs and Token securely. Fill them in below:

requestedEnvs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
requestedEnvs.description: Your GitHub personal access token (classic or fine-grained)

requestedEnvs.name: GITHUB_REPO_URLS
requestedEnvs.description: Your GitHub repository URLs (comma-separated if multiple)

output.reason: You asked to share your GitHub URLs and Token securely. Fill them in below:

responseMessageContents.inputArgsStr: {"reason": "You asked to share your GitHub URLs and Token securely. Fill them in below:", "envs": [{"name": "GITHUB_TOKEN", "description": "Your GitHub personal access token (classic or fine-grained)", "placeholder": "ghp_...", "isPublic": false}, {"name": "GITHUB_REPO_URLS", "description": "Your GitHub repository URLs (comma-separated if multiple)", "placeholder": "https://github.com/user/repo1, https://github.com/user/repo2", "isPublic": false}]}
